text.title: Status Reporting and Integrated Change Control
text.summary: Deterministic RAG computation rules from EVM metrics, change control workflow as git PRs, and integration antipatterns.

section.title: RAG Computation Rules

rule.statement: Compute RAG from measurable thresholds, never from opinion. Schedule: GREEN if SPI >= 0.95, YELLOW 0.85-0.95, RED below 0.85. Cost: GREEN if CPI >= 0.95, YELLOW 0.85-0.95, RED below 0.85. Overall: worst single dimension.
rule.rationale: Opinion-based status defaults to GREEN because pessimistic framing is rarer in training data and in human psychology. Threshold-based computation removes the bias.

rule.statement: Missing data is YELLOW with reason, not GREEN. If a required metric is null, surface it as a gap rather than assuming everything is on track.
rule.rationale: LLMs return GREEN when data is absent; the correct response is to flag the missing input as a monitoring gap requiring investigation.

section.reference: Deterministic RAG from EVM + risk YAML baselines

rule.statement: Every change request must traverse all 8 component plans (scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk, resources, comms, procurement) and emit an impact matrix before approval. Standalone scope edits without schedule and cost re-estimates are rejected.
rule.rationale: Silo approval of scope changes is the primary mechanism by which integration fails. A scope addition that "won't affect timeline" always affects timeline — the PM just hasn't computed it yet.

rule.statement: Run change control as a PR: CR description, cross-area impact matrix, baseline patch proposal, sponsor approval, merge tagged baseline-vN+1. Every baseline change must have a unique CR ID and immutable diff in git history.
rule.rationale: Audit trails are mandatory in regulated programs. A git-based change control system provides immutable history with timestamps and approvers.

rule.statement: Agents have zero authority to approve baseline changes. The agent proposes baseline patches; the sponsor or change board approves via their recorded sign-off before merge.
rule.rationale: Autonomous baseline changes by agents constitute an audit risk and a governance failure. Human approval is non-negotiable for any change touching cost or schedule baselines.

antipattern.description: Component plans in heterogeneous tools (Jira for tasks, Smartsheet for schedule, Excel for budget) without a normalised export layer.
antipattern.reason: Without a common format, the integration agent cannot compute cross-area impact. Require each plan owner to export to a canonical YAML schema at each status cycle.

antipattern.description: Status report generation that leaks vendor pricing, customer names, or employee performance into a shared report.
antipattern.reason: Component plans often contain commercially sensitive and personally identifiable information. Run a scrubber on the integration report before publishing to broad channels.
